pattern: Only values that directly follow an occurrence of key ever matter, so a single pass tallying those followers reduces the problem to picking the most common entry in a small frequency table.
hints: Scan adjacent pairs of the array and only pay attention to a pair when its first element equals key.
Keep a running tally of how often each value shows up immediately after key, ignoring every value that never follows key.
The answer is guaranteed unique, so once you have the tally, just report whichever value has the highest count.

edge_inputs.description: two candidate followers, one clearly more frequent
key's last occurrence is the final element and has no follower
several distinct followers with one clear unique winner
values at the maximum allowed magnitude

stress.description: random array engineered so key appears often, with a random target biased to be frequent
stress.note: the generator biases toward a single dominant follower to keep the 'unique max' precondition realistic
